data.title: S&P 500 sector weekly ETF flows: inflow resumes, bitcoin gains on higher inflows
data.description:
Investors were overall net purchasers of 11 S&P 500 sector ETFs, including SPDR S&P 500 Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY) after redeeming funds in the prior week.

The past week, ending December 06, saw net inflows of $7.08B, with SPY adding $6.17B to its AUM, reversing its outflow of $1.37B in the earlier week.

Bitcoin ETF Flows iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) saw an inflow of $908M over the week, higher than the $781M inflow in the prior week. Bitcoin finally surpassed the $100K level after languishing below that milestone for nearly a month.

Index performance

At the close of the week, U.S. benchmark indexes marked two big milestones: the S&P 500 (SP500) surged past 6,100 points in a historic first, and the benchmark index has now smashed past the year-end targets of most of the brokerages on the Street.

For the week, the S&P climbed +1.0%, while the Nasdaq Composite added +3.3%. The Dow slipped 0.6%.

Last week’s inflows/ outflows:

The investing trend has been similar for the past 2 weeks with financials, consumer staples, industrial, materials, utilities, real estate and communication services reportedly led the buying activity, except for consumer discretionary fund which saw an outflow of $383.74M compared to inflow of $275.94M in the prior week.

Financials recorded the highest inflow among the sectors, attracting $842.1M in inflows, followed by consumer staples which had inflows of $543M in the last week.

Breakdown of S&P 500 sector fund flows (highest to lowest):

Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF): $842.16M.
Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP): $543M.
Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLI): $248.4M.
Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLB): $184.3M.
Utilities Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLU): $81.8M.
Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE): $36M.
Communication Services Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLC): $25M.
Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV): -$67M.
Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK): -$214.6M.
Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE): -$382.3M.
Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY): -$383.7M.
Commodities and Bitcoin ETF fund flows:
U.S. Oil Fund, LP ETF (USO): $28.4M.
Gold SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD): $(562.2M).
Silver iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV): $29.6M.
Bitcoin ETF Flows iShares Bitcoin Trust Registered (IBIT): $908.01M.
ProShares Short Bitcoin Strategy ETF (BITI): $3.89M

data.title: Synopsys offers remedies in EU review of planned $35B Ansys takeover
data.description:
Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) has offered remedies to the European Union to address the regulator's antitrust concerns about the $35 billion planned takeover of engineering software maker Ansys (ANSS).

The European Commission has set a Jan. 10 deadline to make a decision if it will move the transaction into an in-depth review, according to a filing on the regulator's website on Tuesday. The specifics of the remedies offered weren't disclosed.

The deal is also the subject of a review by the U.K.'s antitrust agency. The Competition and Markets Authority set a deadline to for a phase 1 decision for Dec. 20.

Synopsys announced in January that it agreed to buy software developer Ansys for about $35B in cash and stock. Under the terms of the deal, Ansys shareholders will receive $197 in cash and 0.3450 shares of Synopsys common stock.

Shares of Synopsys (SNPS) fell 1.5% on Tuesday, while Ansys (ANSS) edged lower by 0.8%.

data.title: Akoya Biosciences and NeraCare enter into a license agreement
data.description:
• Akoya Biosciences (NASDAQ:AKYA) and NeraCare announced an exclusive global license agreement to develop and commercialize NeraCare’s Immunoprint test on Akoya's multiplexed immunofluorescent platform.
  • The license agreement grants Akoya the exclusive right to develop, market and commercialize the test for clinical research, diagnostic development or, following regulatory approval, patient clinical testing as both a laboratory test or a distributable diagnostic on Akoya's PhenoImager HT platform.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: VIP Play names new technology chief
data.description:
• Online gaming operator VIP Play (OTCPK:VIPZ) appointed Fernando Daniel Goldstein as its chief technology officer to lead the development of the new VIP Play mobile gambling app, which VIP Play expects to launch in early 2025 in Tennessee and additional US markets.
  • Daniel, an experienced CTO, brings experience in various highly regulated markets within the U.S. and globally. 
  • Prior to joining the VIP Play, Inc. team as CTO, he served as VP of Engineering at Qenta.

data.title: SL Green Realty upgraded on improving New York City office fundamentals
data.description:
SL Green Realty (NYSE:SLG) was trading higher after J.P.Morgan upgraded the office REIT on improving New York City office fundamentals.

The stock was up 0.67% Tuesday pre-market at $76.95.

"New York City office fundamentals are improving at a solid pace, though it has not been even for all buildings in the recovery. The company has gone on the offensive in terms of investing, and it appears there is much more to come on this front," said the financial services firm in a research note.

SLG provided guidance for 2025 earnings that was about 4% higher than expected, and JPM raised its numbers. The financial services firm revised its 2025 FFO/share estimate upwards to $5.43 from $5.19.

JPM upgraded the stock to Neutral from Underweight, and increased the year-end 2025 price target to $80.00 from $51.00.

"We think there remains runway for the company's internal growth to improve in the next few years, and it has the capital costs and access to capital to make accretive investments at a time where properties being available for sale should increase in the office segment – and in NYC particularly – after several years of limited deal activity," said the note.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant rating of Hold. SA authors give the stock a Buy rating.

data.title: American Express aims for 9% revenue growth this year, CEO says
data.description:
"We've been really good from a credit perspective," American Express (NYSE:AXP) Chairman and CEO Stephen J. Squeri said Tuesday at the Goldman Sachs U.S. Financial Services conference.

The company aims for 9% revenue growth this year, he noted. "10% is the correct aspiration."

"Spending has been relatively stable," he added.

An environment with not a lot of inflation and taxes would be helpful to increase corporate spending. He expects a better of "line of sight" with the new White House administration.

"Scale gives us lots of opportunities from a leverage perspective," Squeri said, noting that the company has been focusing on Millennials and Gen Z. "We continue to retain customers and to acquire new customers."

Lending needs to grow slightly more than billings, Squeri said. "I'm very confident we'll continue to grow at mid-teens (percentage) EPS."

For the last four or five quarters, AXP billings have been growing at about 6%, he said.

American Express (AXP) stock rose 0.2% at ~9:33 AM ET.

Updated at 9:34 AM ET: Spending at restaurants has been booming, Squeri said.

Breaking... check back for updates.

data.title: SolarMax Technology signs MOU for cryptocurrency solar projects
data.description:
• SolarMax Technology (NASDAQ:SMXT) entered into a Memorandum of Understanding with SOS Limited (NYSE:SOS) to develop large-scale solar power and battery storage facilities in Nevada, Texas and Florida to provide power for SOS’ blockchain and cryptocurrency operations.
  • The MOU contemplates the development of six separate facilities, each with a capacity of approximately 50 megawatts, for a combined total of approximately 300 megawatts.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. finalizes $6.1B+ Chips Act funding with Micron
data.description:
Update: Adds share movement and additional information from Department of Commerce release.

The U.S. Department of Commerce has finalized one of its largest awards to date with memory chipmaker Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU), the White House said in a statement on Tuesday.

The Boise, Idaho-based company had announced a preliminary memorandum of terms with the Department in April for up to ~$6.14B in direct funding under the CHIPS and Science Act.

The $6.165B award finalized today will support the construction of two fabs in Clay, New York, and one fab in Boise, Idaho as part of Micron’s (MU) total $125B investment across both states over the next two decades.

The Department will disburse the funds based on Micron’s completion of project milestones.

Additionally, the commerce department has agreed to preliminary terms with Micron (MU) for an additional $275M investment to expand and modernize its Manassas, Virginia facility and onshore a critical technology relied upon by the country’s defense industry, automotive sector, and national security community.

The expected capital expenditure for the modernization will be $2B over the next several years.

“These investments will help the U.S. grow its share of advanced memory manufacturing from nearly 0% today to 10% over the next decade,” Vice President Kamala Harris said.

The CHIPS Act provides for $39B in grants, billions more in loans and 25% tax credits to boost domestic chip manufacturing and reduce reliance on Asia.

Shares of Micron Technology (MU) rose 2.6% premarket on Tuesday.

The commerce department has announced preliminary deals with more than 20 companies and has finalized agreements with some companies, including Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Intel (INTC), GlobalFoundries (GFS) and Entegris (ENTG). The Biden administration is racing to get the deals finalized before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.

data.title: Sandstorm Gold renews credit facility and enters automatic share purchase plan
data.description:
• Sandstorm Gold (NYSE:SAND) entered into an automatic share purchase plan with its designated broker to facilitate the purchase of the company's shares under its previously announced normal course issuer bid.
  • The ASPP will facilitate purchases under the NCIB at times when the company would ordinarily not be permitted to make purchases, whether due to regulatory restriction or customary self-imposed blackout periods.
  • The company has renewed its revolving credit agreement allowing the company to borrow up to $625M. The amounts drawn on the facility are subject to interest at SOFR plus 1.75%–2.75% per annum.
  • The revised interest rates above SOFR represents a 75-basis point reduction at the upper end and a reduction of 12.5 basis points at the lower end when compared to the previous credit agreement.
  • The facility has a term of four years, maturing in December 2028.
